days.title: Not Quite Lisp
days.essence: Track a running floor counter from '(' and ')' characters; part 2 asks for the first index that drops the counter to -1. Pure warmup to learn the I/O format.

days.title: I Was Told There Would Be No Math
days.essence: Per-box arithmetic on l x w x h dimensions: surface area plus smallest side for paper, then smallest-perimeter plus volume for ribbon. Straightforward formula evaluation over each line.

days.title: Perfectly Spherical Houses in a Vacuum
days.essence: Walk a 2D grid following arrow directions and count distinct visited cells via a set; part 2 alternates moves between Santa and Robo-Santa and unions both trails.

days.title: The Ideal Stocking Stuffer
days.essence: Brute-force the smallest integer suffix whose MD5 of input+n starts with five (then six) leading zeros. Part 2 simply demands more leading zeros, raising the search cost.

days.title: Doesn't He Have Intern-Elves For This?
days.essence: Classify strings as nice/naughty by counting vowels, repeated letters and banned substrings; part 2 swaps in trickier rules about non-overlapping pairs and a letter repeated with one gap.

days.title: Probably a Fire Hazard
days.essence: Apply on/off/toggle rectangle commands to a 1000x1000 light grid and count lit lights; part 2 reinterprets the same commands as brightness deltas and sums total brightness.

days.title: Some Assembly Required
days.essence: Evaluate a circuit of 16-bit bitwise gates by lazily recursing on each wire's inputs and caching results; part 2 feeds wire a's value into b and re-evaluates the whole circuit.

days.title: Matchsticks
days.essence: Compute the difference between code length and the in-memory decoded length of escaped string literals; part 2 instead counts how many characters re-encoding (re-escaping) would add.

days.title: All in a Single Night
days.essence: Find the shortest (then longest) route visiting every city exactly once - a Travelling-Salesman / Hamiltonian-path search over a small weighted graph, solved here by branch-and-bound over orderings.

days.title: Elves Look, Elves Say
days.essence: Repeatedly apply the look-and-say run-length transform and report the resulting length; the string grows exponentially, so part 2's extra iterations (40 then 50) push naive string handling toward its limit.

days.title: Corporate Policy
days.essence: Increment an 8-letter password like a base-26 odometer (skipping i/o/l) until it satisfies a straight, no-banned-letters and two-pairs rule set; part 2 just finds the next valid password after that.

days.title: JSAbacusFramework.io
days.essence: Sum every number in a JSON document; part 1 is a regex scan, but part 2 requires recursively walking the parsed structure and discarding any object (and its subtree) containing the value 'red'.

days.title: Knights of the Dinner Table
days.essence: Maximize total happiness over a circular seating arrangement given pairwise like/dislike weights by trying all permutations; part 2 adds yourself as a neutral guest, enlarging the permutation space.

days.title: Reindeer Olympics
days.essence: Reindeer alternate fly/rest cycles; part 1 uses a closed-form distance at t=2503, but part 2 must step second-by-second to award a point to every current leader and report the top score.

days.title: Science for Hungry People
days.essence: Distribute 100 teaspoons among ingredients to maximize the product of clamped property totals, enumerated via recursive integer compositions; part 2 adds a hard constraint that the recipe total exactly 500 calories.

days.title: Aunt Sue
days.essence: Identify the one Aunt Sue whose partially-known attributes match the MFCSAM readout; part 2 reinterprets cats/trees as greater-than and pomeranians/goldfish as less-than constraints rather than equalities.

days.title: No Such Thing as Too Much
days.essence: Count how many subsets of distinct-id containers sum to exactly 150 (a subset-sum enumeration); part 2 finds the minimum number of containers used and counts how many subsets achieve it.

days.title: Like a GIF For Your Yard
days.essence: Run Conway's Game of Life for 100 generations on a light grid and count lit cells; part 2 forces the four corners permanently on, slightly changing the steady state.

days.title: Medicine for Rudolph
days.essence: Part 1 counts distinct molecules after one forward grammar replacement; part 2 wants the fewest replacements to build the molecule from 'e', for which a naive reverse-BFS (as attempted here) never finishes and the intended solution exploits the grammar structure (greedy reduction / token counting).

days.title: Infinite Elves and Infinite Houses
days.essence: Each house n receives 10x the sum of its divisors, so find the first house exceeding the target by sieving divisor contributions; part 2 caps each elf to 50 houses and bumps the per-elf gift to 11.

days.title: RPG Simulator 20XX
days.essence: Enumerate every legal weapon/armor/ring loadout from the shop and decide each deterministic fight by comparing required hit counts; part 1 finds the cheapest winning set, part 2 the most expensive losing one.

days.title: Wizard Simulator 20XX
days.essence: Find the least mana to win a turn-based wizard fight with timed effect spells (shield, poison, recharge); it is a minimum-cost search over fiddly combat states, with part 2 adding 1 self-damage each player turn on hard mode.

days.title: Opening the Turing Lock
days.essence: Implement a tiny register machine with six instructions (hlf, tpl, inc, jmp, jie, jio) and report register b after halting; part 2 just changes the initial value of register a.

days.title: It Hangs in the Balance
days.essence: Partition the packages into three (then four) equal-weight groups, minimizing the first group's size and breaking ties by smallest quantum entanglement (product of weights); the trick is enumerating only minimal-size subsets summing to the target weight.

days.title: Let It Snow
days.essence: Convert the (row, column) target into its 1-based diagonal index via triangular numbers, then advance the code by repeated modular multiplication (x252533 mod 33554393) from the seed - a closed-form/modular-arithmetic puzzle and the traditional single-star finale.
